items.name: deep-learning-model-scaling-analysis
items.description: Causal analysis framework using Double Machine Learning to quantitatively isolate the effect of model size on deep learning performance while controlling for confounders such as dataset size, training time, and hyperparameters.

items.name: LEMONsc
items.description: Framework for  mass estimation of massive objects in astrophysical collision-driven systems using machine learning models

items.name: MegaPlantTF
items.description: A Machine Learning Framework for Comprehensive Classification of Plant Transcription Factors

items.name: Malware_Detection
items.description: This project is a web based malware detection system developed using machine learning and the Streamlit framework. It analyzes uploaded files and predicts whether a file is safe or malware using a Random Forest model trained on Windows PE file features for academic demonstration purposes.

items.name: Project-A-Z-Cognitive-Pipeline-End-to-End-Autonomous-ML-Engineering-by-Gemini
items.description: A high-performance ML ecosystem transforming Udemy’s Machine Learning A-Z into a production-ready framework. Features a dynamic preprocessing engine, a "tournament-style" model selector across 20+ algorithms, and an automated deployment layer. Designed to outperform static pipelines through modular design and intelligent automation.

items.name: cloud-cost-prediction-idle-detection-ml
items.description: Machine Learning framework for cloud cost prediction and idle resource detection.

items.name: HIT-OR-FLOP
items.description: A machine-learning framework analyzing audio features, genre, and lyrics to predict and model song popularity

items.name: AI-Driven-Framework-for-Noise-Resilient-and-Application-Oriented-Quantum-Computing-in-the-NISQ-Era
items.description: This project develops an AI-driven framework to enhance noise resilience and optimize application-oriented quantum computing in the NISQ era. By integrating machine learning for error mitigation, circuit optimization, and hybrid quantum-classical workflows, it enables reliable, practical quantum solutions within current hardware limitations.

items.name: SmartHealth-AI
items.description: Health Insurance Cost Prediction App  This web app, which is built with the Streamlit web development framework, uses a machine learning regression algorithm for the purpose of health insurance cost predictions, taking into consideration the personal and lifestyle characteristics of the individual.

items.name: dvshriram-dvs
items.description: Hi, I’m D V Shriram, I am an aspiring AI professional passionate about machine learning, data science, and intelligent systems. With a strong foundation in IT fundamentals such as networking, and system troubleshooting, I am now focused on Python programming, machine learning algorithms, and deep learning frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch.

items.name: Diabetes_Prediction_using_Machine_Learning
items.description: End-to-end machine learning framework for diabetes prediction. Leverages Local Outlier Factor (LOF) for anomaly detection and 10-fold cross-validation for model selection. Features an optimized ensemble of LightGBM and Gradient Boosting tuned via GridSearchCV for maximum sensitivity.

items.name: matbench-discovery
items.description: An evaluation framework for machine learning models simulating high-throughput materials discovery.

items.name: caretSDM
items.description: caretSDM applies a geoprocessing framework to the caret machine learning package, facilitating the access of Species Distribution Modeling community to state-of-the-art methods.

items.description: An explainable, high-performance intrusion detection framework evaluating black-box machine learning models using SHAP and LIME.

items.name: Plant-s-Doctor
items.description: An end-to-end machine learning based model that predicts the diseases of different plants from the picture of the leaf and provides the solution for that disease. Built using Flask framework.

items.name: NN_regularization
items.description: This repository provides the numerical framework for solving ill-posed inverse problems using a regularization strategy based on the parametrization of the solution via a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). Unlike typical machine learning approaches, this method does not require training data and interprets the MLP as an implicit neural representation.

items.name: Beyond-Credit-Scores-Loan-Default-Prediction
items.description: This project builds a machine learning framework to improve loan default prediction by combining traditional credit attributes with real-world transaction behavior. Instead of treating borrowers as static profiles, the model captures dynamic financial patterns such as spending volatility, activity frequency, and temporal behavioral shifts.

items.name: GLA-V1
items.description: GLA BUILDER is a local, natural‑language software builder that creates websites, applications, and digital tools end‑to‑end. It runs per machine, integrates frameworks like Node.js, Rust, and Python, and features persistent memory, self‑learning, and stress‑free automation.

items.name: MLPotion

Think of the library as a set of building blocks—atoms, steps, and components—that you can mix, match, and sequence to build a fully customized training or inference pipeline.

I compare traditional actuarial approaches (Logistic Regression) with modern machine learning models including Decision Trees, Random Forest, XGBoost, and a PyTorch-based Neural Network.
